title: Homalolepis docensis
intro: Homalolepis docensis, also known as the Mexican chamomile or Yerba Buena, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it a natural remedy for treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Homalolepis docensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Homalolepis docensis is classified within the family Rutaceae and is a species of flowering plant. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its classification also places it within the genus Homalolepis, which contains only a few species. Further, it is a member of the order Sapindales, a group of plants that also includes citrus and rue species. As a member of the Rutaceae family, Homalolepis docensis is closely related to other plants in this family, such as citrus and orange species.
